task: I’m putting together a short note about a paperwork mismatch on a vendor order, and I want it to keep the discussion tied to the documents. Can you draft something I can send, with a blank where I can drop in the exact reference or amount?

turns.agent_response:
Sure — here’s a short, document-focused draft with a blank you can fill in:

**Subject:** Paperwork Mismatch on Order [Order #]

Hi [Vendor Name],

We found a mismatch in the paperwork for order [Order #]. Please review and send the corrected documents for **[reference/amount]** so we can keep this tied to the order records.

We’ll hold the order until the paperwork matches.

Thanks,  
[Your Name]

If you want, I can make it even shorter or more firm.

user_simulator.text:
Close, but make it firmer and less chatty. Keep it strictly on the record trail. Something like:

Subject: Paperwork Mismatch on Order [Order #]

Hi [Vendor Name],

There is a mismatch in the paperwork for order [Order #]. Please review and provide corrected documents for [reference/amount] so the file matches the order record.

We will not release further action until the documents are corrected.

[Your Name]

That’s the tone I’d use. Tight, no extra wording.

turns.agent_response:
Yes — that’s tight and record-focused. Here’s a slightly cleaner version you can send:

**Subject:** Paperwork Mismatch on Order [Order #]

Hi [Vendor Name],

There is a mismatch in the paperwork for order [Order #]. Please review and provide corrected documents for [reference/amount] so the file matches the order record.

We will not release further action until the documents are corrected.

[Your Name]

If you want, I can make it even firmer or more formal.

user_simulator.text: Yeah. That works. Tight enough, and it keeps it on the record trail.
